\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Circuit Tree Manip}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.LMN.CircuitTreeManip
\section{Overview}

This module provides infrastructure for navigating and transforming the \lean{Circuit}
tree type used in the LMN layer-reduction argument. It supplies De~Morgan dual
conversions between DNF and CNF, structural facts about circuit depth, and the
core ``absorb one level'' operation that folds the top level of a circuit into the
Layer~2 gates, lowering the circuit depth by at least one while keeping every gate
representable by a width-$l$ DNF and CNF.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

\begin{definition}[DNF to dual CNF]
\lean{LMN.dnfToDualCNF}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, DNF, Literal.flipNeg}
The De~Morgan dual of a DNF $\varphi$ on $n$ variables, obtained by negating every
literal of every term and reading the result as a CNF. Concretely
$\neg\big(\bigvee_i \bigwedge_j \ell_{ij}\big) = \bigwedge_i \bigvee_j \neg\ell_{ij}$.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Dual CNF preserves width]
\lean{LMN.dnfToDualCNF_width}
\leanok
\uses{CNF.width, DNF, DNF.width, LMN.dnfToDualCNF, Term.width}
The dual CNF $\mathrm{dnfToDualCNF}\,\varphi$ has the same width as $\varphi$, since
flipping the sign of each literal leaves the number of literals per term (and hence
per clause) unchanged.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Dual CNF negates the evaluation]
\lean{LMN.dnfToDualCNF_eval}
\leanok
\uses{CNF.eval, CNF.evalClause, DNF, DNF.eval, LMN.dnfToDualCNF, Literal.flipNeg_eval, Term.eval}
For every input $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the dual CNF evaluates to the
negation of the original DNF: $\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,(\mathrm{dnfToDualCNF}\,\varphi)\,x
= \neg(\mathrm{DNF.eval}\,\varphi\,x)$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Depth $\geq 1$ forces a node]
\lean{LMN.Circuit.exists_node_of_depth_ge_one}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth}
A circuit $c$ with $\mathrm{depth}\,c \geq 1$ is not a literal: there exist a Boolean
flag $\mathit{isAnd}$ and a child list $cs$ with $c = \mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Depth-1 node has depth-0 children]
\lean{LMN.Circuit.depth1_children_are_lits}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth}
If a node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has depth at most $1$, then every
child $c \in cs$ has depth $0$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Depth-0 circuit is a literal]
\lean{LMN.Circuit.depth0_is_lit}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Lit}
A circuit $c$ with $\mathrm{depth}\,c = 0$ is a literal: there exists $lr : \mathrm{Lit}\,m$
with $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,lr$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Depth-1 node has literal children]
\lean{LMN.Circuit.depth1_all_lits}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Lit, LMN.Circuit.depth0_is_lit, LMN.Circuit.depth1_children_are_lits}
If a node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has depth at most $1$, then every
child $c \in cs$ is a literal, i.e.\ $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,lr$ for some $lr$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Child function under gate substitution]
\lean{LMN.childFunction}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval}
The Boolean function on $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$ computed by a subcircuit
$c_{\mathrm{sub}}$ when each of its inputs $i$ is replaced by the gate function
$\mathrm{gates}\,i$, namely $x \mapsto c_{\mathrm{sub}}.\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Switched gates have DNF and CNF]
\lean{LMN.switched_gates_have_dnf_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.cleanDNF, LMN.cleanDNF_eval, LMN.cleanDNF_nodup, LMN.cleanDNF_var_inj, LMN.cleanDNF_width_le, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
If, after applying a restriction $\rho$, every gate function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{gates}\,i).\mathrm{eval}\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth at
most $l$, then each gate admits both a width-$l$ DNF representation (whose terms are
duplicate-free and contain no two literals on the same variable) and a width-$l$ CNF
representation, each computing the same function.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[OR of literal children has a width-$l$ DNF]
\lean{LMN.or_of_lit_children_dnf}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Lit, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.compression_or_of_dnfs, cnfToDualDNF, cnfToDualDNF_eval, cnfToDualDNF_width}
Consider an OR node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathrm{false}\,cs$ whose children are all
literals, where each gate function has a width-$l$ DNF and a width-$l$ CNF
representation. Then the function computed by the node also has a width-$l$ DNF
$\varphi$ with $\varphi.\mathrm{eval}\,x = (\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathrm{false}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ for all $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[AND of literal children has a width-$l$ CNF]
\lean{LMN.and_of_lit_children_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Lit, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.compression_and_of_cnfs, LMN.dnfToDualCNF, LMN.dnfToDualCNF_eval, LMN.dnfToDualCNF_width}
Consider an AND node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs$ whose children are all
literals, where each gate function has a width-$l$ DNF and a width-$l$ CNF
representation. Then the function computed by the node has a width-$l$ CNF $\psi$ with
$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,\psi\,x = (\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ for all $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Absorb a depth-1 top circuit]
\lean{LMN.absorbOneLevel_depth1}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.Circuit.depth1_all_lits, LMN.Circuit.exists_node_of_depth_ge_one, LMN.Layer2Data, LMN.and_of_lit_children_cnf, LMN.cleanDNF, LMN.cleanDNF_eval, LMN.cleanDNF_nodup, LMN.cleanDNF_var_inj, LMN.cleanDNF_width_le, LMN.or_of_lit_children_dnf, cnfToDualDNF, cnfToDualDNF_eval, cnfToDualDNF_width}
Let $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ have depth exactly $1$ over the gates of some $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$,
where (with $0 < l$) each gate has a width-$l$ DNF and CNF representation. Then there is a
new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$ of depth
$0$ over it computing the same function as $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ over the original gates.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Depth-$\leq 1$ child has a signed width-$l$ DNF]
\lean{LMN.child_depth_le1_has_signed_dnf}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Lit, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.Circuit.depth1_all_lits, LMN.Circuit.exists_node_of_depth_ge_one, LMN.and_of_lit_children_cnf, LMN.cleanDNF, LMN.cleanDNF_eval, LMN.cleanDNF_nodup, LMN.cleanDNF_var_inj, LMN.cleanDNF_width_le, LMN.or_of_lit_children_dnf, cnfToDualDNF, cnfToDualDNF_eval, cnfToDualDNF_width}
A circuit $c_j$ of depth at most $1$ over gates that each have a width-$l$ DNF and CNF
representation admits a width-$l$ DNF $\varphi$ together with a sign bit such that, for
all $x$, the signed evaluation $\big(\mathrm{if}\ \mathit{sign}\ \mathrm{then}\
\varphi.\mathrm{eval}\,x\ \mathrm{else}\ \neg\varphi.\mathrm{eval}\,x\big)$ equals
$c_j.\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$; moreover the terms of $\varphi$
are duplicate-free with no repeated variable.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Signed DNFs for a list of children]
\lean{LMN.list_child_signed_dnfs}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.child_depth_le1_has_signed_dnf}
Given a list $cs$ of circuits each of depth at most $1$, over gates with width-$l$ DNF and
CNF representations, there are families $\varphi_s$ of width-$l$ DNFs (with duplicate-free,
non-repeating-variable terms) and signs indexed by $\mathrm{Fin}\,cs.\mathrm{length}$ such
that, for each index $j$, the signed evaluation of $\varphi_s\,j$ matches
$(cs.\mathrm{get}\,j).\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ on every $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Build a node of signed literals]
\lean{LMN.build_literal_circuit}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval}
For any flag $\mathit{isAnd}$, arity $k$, and sign family $\mathit{signs} : \mathrm{Fin}\,k
\to \mathrm{Bool}$, there is a circuit $c'$ of depth at most $1$ that equals the node
$\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}$ over the literals $\mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,\langle j,
\mathit{signs}\,j\rangle$, evaluating identically on every input assignment $g$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Depth reduction for depth-1 circuits]
\lean{LMN.exists_circuit_depth_reduction_depth1}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.child_depth_le1_has_signed_dnf}
A circuit $c$ of depth exactly $1$ over gates with width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations
collapses to a single signed gate: there exist a width-$l$ DNF $\varphi$ (with
duplicate-free, non-repeating-variable terms) and a sign bit whose signed evaluation
equals $c.\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ for all $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Depth reduction for depth-2 circuits]
\lean{LMN.exists_circuit_depth_reduction_depth2}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.Circuit.exists_node_of_depth_ge_one, LMN.build_literal_circuit, LMN.list_child_signed_dnfs}
A circuit $c$ of depth exactly $2$ over gates with width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations
reduces to a circuit $c'$ of depth at most $1$ over a fresh gate family $\mathrm{gates}'$
of width-$l$ DNFs (with duplicate-free, non-repeating-variable terms), so that $c$ and
$c'$ compute the same function on every $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Merge child reduction results]
\lean{LMN.reduce_children}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width}
Given a list $cs$ of circuits where each child $cs.\mathrm{get}\,j$ has been reduced to a
circuit $c_j$ of depth at most $\mathrm{bound}\,j$ over its own width-$l$ DNF gate family,
this combines all the per-child gate families into a single merged family
$\mathrm{merged}$ indexed by $\mathrm{Fin}\,M$, with re-indexed circuits $\mathrm{new\_cs}\,j$
of depth at most $\mathrm{bound}\,j$ that reproduce each child's function.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Node evaluation under finRange re-indexing]
\lean{LMN.node_eval_eq_of_finRange_map}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval}
If each re-indexed child $\mathrm{new\_cs}\,j$ evaluated at $g'$ agrees with the original
child $cs.\mathrm{get}\,j$ evaluated at $g$, then the node over $cs$ at $g$ equals the node
over $(\mathrm{List.finRange}\,cs.\mathrm{length}).\mathrm{map}\,\mathrm{new\_cs}$ at $g'$,
for the same flag $\mathit{isAnd}$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[General circuit depth reduction]
\lean{LMN.exists_circuit_depth_reduction}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Lit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.Circuit.exists_node_of_depth_ge_one, LMN.child_depth_le1_has_signed_dnf, LMN.exists_circuit_depth_reduction_depth1, LMN.exists_circuit_depth_reduction_depth2, LMN.node_eval_eq_of_finRange_map, LMN.reduce_children}
For a circuit $c$ of depth at least $1$ (with $0 < l$) over gates that each have a width-$l$
DNF representation with duplicate-free, non-repeating-variable terms and a width-$l$ CNF
representation, there exist a fresh gate family $\mathrm{gates}'$ of such width-$l$ DNFs and
a circuit $c'$ of depth at most $\mathrm{depth}\,c - 1$ computing the same function as $c$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Absorb a general top circuit (depth $\geq 2$)]
\lean{LMN.absorbOneLevel_general}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.Layer2Data, LMN.exists_circuit_depth_reduction}
The $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$ wrapper of the general depth reduction: for $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ of
depth at least $2$ (with $0 < l$) over gates having width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations,
there are a new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$
of depth at most $\mathrm{depth}\,c_{\mathrm{top}} - 1$ computing the same function.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Absorb one level of the top circuit]
\lean{LMN.absorbOneLevel}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.Layer2Data, LMN.absorbOneLevel_depth1, LMN.absorbOneLevel_general}
The combined absorption step: for $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ of depth at least $1$ (with $0 < l$)
over $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$ gates having width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations, there are a
new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$ of depth
at most $\mathrm{depth}\,c_{\mathrm{top}} - 1$ computing the same function. This dispatches
to the depth-1 case (giving depth $0$) and the general case (giving depth at most $1$).
\end{lemma}
